apsNote: Engineering and Health Sciences close earlier. Check faculty pages.
requiredDocuments: Certified copy of South African ID or valid passport
Certified copy of matric certificate or statement of results
Proof of residence (not older than 3 months)

internationalStudents.note: International applicants require SAQA evaluation of qualifications.

applicationCloses.note: 30 June 2026 for Health Sciences, Architecture, Audiology, Speech-Language Pathology, BA Film & TV

minimumAPS.note: 34 general minimum; competitive programmes much higher

apsSystem: APS /56 (best 7 subjects including Life Orientation; English and Mathematics attract bonus points)

flags: feeIntl: The R100 application fee was confirmed for SA applicants; the international applicants page did not specify the same R100 fee — international students pay a separate non-refundable International Registration Fee of R6,970 at registration, but the application fee itself is not separately stated for international applicants on official pages
Medicine/MBBCh APS set to 0 because admission uses a Composite Index (60% matric average of 5 subjects + 40% NBT score), not a standard APS threshold
LLB 3-year and 2-year streams do not use matric APS — admitted based on undergraduate degree performance
Prospectus URL links to a landing page; the 2027 guide PDF is available for download from that page but a direct PDF URL was not confirmed
applicationOpens confirmed as March 2026 from timelines page — specific day (1 March) sourced from third-party aggregators; official Wits timelines page states only 'March 2026'
BA Film and TV and Bachelor of Architectural Studies also close 30 June 2026 (same as Health Sciences early deadline)
